verses.text: 'Praise ye the LORD,' the psalmist resolves to praise Him 'with my whole heart' among the upright, for His works are great and honourable. Whole-hearted praise befits His deeds. The works of God deserve our undivided thanks.

'Praise ye the LORD,' the psalmist resolves to praise Him 'with my whole heart' among the upright, for His works are great and honourable. Whole-hearted praise befits His deeds. The works of God deserve our undivided thanks.

'Praise ye the LORD,' the psalmist resolves to praise Him 'with my whole heart' among the upright, for His works are great and honourable. Whole-hearted praise befits His deeds. The works of God deserve our undivided thanks.

He has made His wonderful works to be remembered, being 'gracious and full of compassion,' giving food and an inheritance to His people. His mercies are meant to be recalled. God provides for His own and would have them never forget it.

He has made His wonderful works to be remembered, being 'gracious and full of compassion,' giving food and an inheritance to His people. His mercies are meant to be recalled. God provides for His own and would have them never forget it.

He has made His wonderful works to be remembered, being 'gracious and full of compassion,' giving food and an inheritance to His people. His mercies are meant to be recalled. God provides for His own and would have them never forget it.

His works are truth and justice, His commandments sure; He sent redemption and made an everlasting covenant, so 'holy and reverend is his name.' Redemption crowns His works. The God who saves is to be approached with holy awe.

His works are truth and justice, His commandments sure; He sent redemption and made an everlasting covenant, so 'holy and reverend is his name.' Redemption crowns His works. The God who saves is to be approached with holy awe.

His works are truth and justice, His commandments sure; He sent redemption and made an everlasting covenant, so 'holy and reverend is his name.' Redemption crowns His works. The God who saves is to be approached with holy awe.
'The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom'; all who do His commandments have good understanding. Reverence is the root of wisdom. To know and fear God is where all true understanding begins.
